task.title: Temporarily reusing the same IP address on different interfaces

li.p: You are logged in as the root user.
The network interfaces are not configured.

context.p: You can use the virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) feature to temporarily use the same IP address on different interfaces in one server. Use this procedure only for testing purposes, because the configuration is temporary and lost after you reboot the system.

note.p: To enable remote peers to contact both VRF interfaces while reusing the same IP address, the network interfaces must belong to different broadcasting domains. A broadcast domain in a network is a set of nodes which receive broadcast traffic sent by any of them. In most configurations, all nodes connected to the same switch belong to the same broadcasting domain.

step.cmd: Create and configure the first VRF device:

li.p: Create the VRF device and assign it to a routing table. For example, to create a VRF device named blue that is assigned to the 1001 routing table:
li.codeblock: # ip link add dev blue type vrf table 1001
# ip link set dev blue up

li.p: Assign a network device to the VRF device. For example, to add the enp1s0 Ethernet device to the blue VRF device:
li.codeblock: # ip link set dev enp1s0 master blue
# ip link set dev enp1s0 up

li.p: Assign an IP address and subnet mask to the enp1s0 device. For example, to set it to 192.0.2.1/24:

step.cmd: Create and configure the next VRF device:

li.p: Create the VRF device and assign it to a routing table. For example, to create a VRF device named red that is assigned to the 1002 routing table:
li.codeblock: # ip link add dev red type vrf table 1002
# ip link set dev red up

li.p: Assign a network device to the VRF device. For example, to add the enp7s0 Ethernet device to the red VRF device:
li.codeblock: # ip link set dev enp7s0 master red
# ip link set dev enp7s0 up

li.p: Assign the same IP address and subnet mask to the enp7s0 device as you used for enp1s0 in the blue VRF domain:

step.cmd: Optional: Create further VRF devices as described above.
